race_name: Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 85 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA's dead flat paved perfection rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA's dead flat paved perfection forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
The aerobic base that lets you ride dead flat paved perfection at tempo for 85 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
When someone attacks on dead flat paved perfection, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA. Train it until it feels boring.
At Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA's dead flat paved perfection actually does to you.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
2-minute all-out efforts on dead flat paved perfection. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.

race_overlay.heat: February in Doral, Florida, USA — February in Doral delivers balmy highs of 77°F (25°C) and lows around 65°F (18°C), with scant 2 inches of rain. Expect endless sunshine perfect for ripping open roads. Minimal wind risk seals it as cycling paradise. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day. Race-day realities: occasional humidity; urban heat pockets.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Endurance to prepare you for 85 miles of dead flat paved perfection in Doral, Florida, USA.
